title: Fix-first: the model-guard suite is RED and fail-opens as green
action: Repair the guard's own instrument before any guard change is attempted. (1) hooks/test_model_guard.mjs:40-46 copyLib() hand-enumerates ["state.mjs","fsutil.mjs"], but state.mjs now imports claims.mjs and reservations.mjs — the import throws inside the fixture, bee-model-guard.mjs fail-opens (exit 0), and all 18 deny rows report 'allowed' while the suite still reads as passing. Derive the vendored set with readdirSync over the real lib dir, exactly as hooks/test_write_guard.mjs:42-57 and hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs:162-169 already do. (2) Add a sentinel-deny row: one case that MUST be denied, so a universally fail-open run can never read as all-pass. (3) Add the hook suites (hooks/test_model_guard.mjs, hooks/test_write_guard.mjs, hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs) to .bee/config.json commands.verify — the rot survived precisely because no verify chain and no CI ran them. (4) Prove falsifiability once: deliberately break the guard, watch the suite go red, restore. This is critical-patterns.md:6-18 verbatim, in the one file that never got the fix.

truths: hooks/test_model_guard.mjs passes with zero failures on a clean tree
the vendored lib set is derived from the real lib directory, never a hand-kept name list
a sentinel-deny case exists and fails the suite if the guard fail-opens universally
the repo verify command runs the hook suites
key_links: Correct pattern already in repo: hooks/test_write_guard.mjs:42-57
prohibitions: Do not change hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs in this cell — the guard's behavior is not in scope, only the ability to test it
Do not weaken any assertion to make the suite green; a red row is the point
Do not remove the fail-open contract from the hook itself

verify: node hooks/test_model_guard.mjs && node hooks/test_write_guard.mjs && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs

trace.dropped_reason: Rebuilt after the high-risk validating panel: ao-0's verify could never pass (test_hook_contracts is also red), its falsifiability step contradicted its own prohibitions, and its verify-chain edit would have bricked the baseline; ao-s1's question was answerable yes-by-redefinition; ao-s2's binding step and capture shape were unsafe and its acceptance criterion was unsatisfiable on the most likely answer.
